raw_consumer_statement:
On my XX/XX/XXXX Citi Card statement I noticed an unauthorized charge on my account. I promptly notified Citi of this charge that was made to my account on XXXX XXXX, XXXX. I notified them within a couple days of receiving my statement with the unauthorized charge. They investigated the charge and came to the conclusion that it was a valid charge. It was a hotel room reservation that was charged for a " no show. '' They sent me a copy of the reservation and it was made by a person unknown to me from a different state. The hotel was in possession of the name, address, and XXXX honors # of the guest ( not me ). But they continued to charge me for a no show. 
The hotel submitted a form checking a box that said " Documentation to prove the cardholder is in possession of and/or using the merchandise '' and a box that said " signed delivery form, copy of cardholder identification. '' On another form a box was checked saying " card not present. '' On another page the hotel submits information that " XXXX booked your reservation at XXXX XXXX XXXX and the XXXX cancelled your reservation XXXX XXXX XXXX, but the reservation was supposedly was for XX/XX/XXXX. ( why was it booked on XX/XX/XXXX? ) Mostly I am flabbergasted that having faithfully paid thousands of dollars over the XXXX years that I had that Citi card, that Citi would doubt my willingness to pay a relatively small charge. When I contested their findings, they simply told me " too much time had elapsed '' and I had no further recourse. I reported this and contested this in a very timely manner. In summary, I can not feel secure about this company 's ability to protect me from fraud.
